locations.label: Austin
locations.note: Austin uses the official city crime-reports feed published by the Austin Police Department. The source records the highest-level offense per incident and is aggregated here by district and calendar year.

sources.label: City of Austin Open Data: Crime Reports

locations.label: Barcelona
locations.note: Barcelona uses official Spanish Interior Ministry annual crime-balance releases for the municipality of Barcelona. The app combines the verified year-end municipal tables published by the ministry; rate-per-100k is not shown because this source path does not bundle a comparable municipal population series in the same extract.

sources.label: Spanish Interior Ministry balances and reports archive
Spanish Interior Ministry crime statistics portal publications

locations.label: Berlin
locations.note: Berlin uses official Polizei Berlin atlas categories. Some categories are umbrella groups while others are nested subtypes, so the stacks are best read as comparison views rather than deduplicated totals.

sources.label: Polizei Berlin Kriminalitätsatlas
Polizei Berlin PKS archive and historical atlas reports
Current district workbook from the official atlas

locations.label: Chicago
locations.note: Chicago uses the official city open-data crime feed. The dashboard aggregates official district-level incident records to calendar years and maps the major offense groups published in the city dataset.

sources.label: Chicago Data Portal: Crimes 2001 to Present

locations.label: Dallas
locations.note: Dallas uses the official Police Incidents open-data feed published by the city. The dashboard aggregates division-level incident records by calendar year and maps the incident descriptions into a stable set of offense buckets.

sources.label: Dallas Open Data: Police Incidents

locations.label: Frankfurt
locations.note: Frankfurt currently uses official citywide open-data crime series from the City of Frankfurt and the Hessisches Landeskriminalamt. These categories are annual city totals rather than district-level atlas slices.

sources.label: City of Frankfurt open data: crimes by offense type
City of Frankfurt open data: citywide demographic indicators

locations.label: Hamburg
locations.note: Hamburg uses official Polizeiliche Kriminalstatistik yearbooks published by the Hamburg police. The app currently shows a stable citywide subset of annual recorded-crime counts from the official PKS comparison tables.

sources.label: Polizei Hamburg: Polizeiliche Kriminalstatistik service page
Polizei Hamburg PKS yearbook 2024

locations.label: Houston
locations.note: Houston uses the official HPD public NIBRS datasets published as annual CSVs. The dashboard aggregates beat-level incident rows to calendar years using the published NIBRS description field.

sources.label: Houston Police Department: Monthly Crime Data by Street / Neighborhood (Police Beat)

locations.label: London
locations.note: London uses official Metropolitan Police recorded-crime categories aggregated to calendar years. A few categories were renamed over time, and those official aliases are merged where the dataset clearly indicates the same series.

sources.label: MPS Recorded Crime: Geographic Breakdown dataset
ONS borough population estimates via London Datastore
ONS England and Wales local-authority population workbook

locations.label: Los Angeles
locations.note: Los Angeles uses the official LAPD crime open-data feeds. The dashboard aggregates area-level incident records to calendar years and groups the official raw offense descriptions into stable major offense buckets.

sources.label: Los Angeles Open Data: Crime Data from 2010 to 2019
Los Angeles Open Data: Crime Data from 2020 to Present

locations.label: Luton
locations.note: Luton uses official Community Safety Partnership and local-authority crime tables. The series is annual, based on rolling 12-month totals at the year ending each March, and it is citywide rather than neighbourhood-level.

sources.label: ONS recorded crime data at Community Safety Partnership and local authority level
ONS population estimates detailed time series

locations.label: Milan
locations.note: Milan uses official Comune di Milano open-data series for reported crimes and official resident-population series for the rate view. The figures are annual citywide totals, not district-level cuts.

sources.label: Comune di Milano open data: reported crimes 2004-2023
Comune di Milano open data: calculated resident population by sex 1936-2023

locations.label: New York City
locations.chartTitle: New York City Crime by Borough
locations.note: New York City uses the official NYPD complaint open-data feeds. The dashboard aggregates official borough-level complaint records to calendar years and shows a mapped set of major offense groups from the NYPD catalog.

sources.label: NYC Open Data: NYPD Complaint Data Historic
NYC Open Data: NYPD Complaint Data Current

locations.label: Paris
locations.note: Paris uses the official communal crime base published by the French Interior Ministry and SSMSI. The figures are citywide commune totals rather than arrondissement-level series.

sources.label: French Interior Ministry / SSMSI communal crime base

locations.label: Phoenix
locations.note: Phoenix uses the official city crime-data CSV published on the municipal open-data portal. The source is aggregated here by grid and calendar year from the city’s UCR crime-category field.

sources.label: City of Phoenix Open Data: Crime Data

locations.label: Rome
locations.note: Rome uses official Roma Capitale urban-security workbooks. These are annual citywide totals rather than neighborhood slices, with yearly offense-type tables and resident population used for the rate view.

sources.label: Roma Capitale statistics: legality and public safety

locations.label: San Francisco
locations.chartTitle: San Francisco Crime by Police District
locations.note: San Francisco uses the official Police Department incident-reports open-data feed. The dashboard aggregates police-district records to calendar years and shows the major incident categories published in the city dataset.

sources.label: San Francisco Open Data: Police Department Incident Reports 2018 to Present

locations.label: São Paulo
locations.note: São Paulo uses the official São Paulo state public-security quarterly statistical tables, restricted here to the year-end fourth-quarter releases and the citywide Capital column so the series remains annual and comparable across years. Rate-per-100k is not shown because this source path does not currently bundle a matching city population series in the same extract.

sources.label: São Paulo State Public Security Secretariat quarterly statistics archive

locations.label: Seattle
locations.note: Seattle uses the official SPD Crime Data open-data feed, which standardizes incidents to NIBRS-style classifications across the 2008-present window. The dashboard aggregates finalized precinct-level records by year.

sources.label: Seattle Open Data: SPD Crime Data 2008-Present

locations.label: Tokyo
locations.note: Tokyo uses the official Tokyo Statistical Yearbook crime table. The source is published at police-station level and is aggregated here to district and ward totals; rate-per-100k is not shown because this path does not currently bundle a matching population series in the same extract.

sources.label: Tokyo Statistical Yearbook: criminal offenses known to the police by type of crime and police station

locations.label: Valencia
locations.note: Barcelona uses official Spanish Interior Ministry annual crime-balance releases for the municipality of Barcelona. The app combines the verified year-end municipal tables published by the ministry; rate-per-100k is not shown because this source path does not bundle a comparable municipal population series in the same extract.

sources.label: Spanish Interior Ministry balances and reports archive
Spanish Interior Ministry crime statistics portal publications
